TASK-001.title: Lock product intent in specs
TASK-001.summary: Turn codewiki intent into explicit product and system docs.

goal.outcome: Project intent and ownership boundaries are explicit enough to guide implementation.
acceptance: Foundational specs describe desired outcomes and major constraints.
At least one roadmap task links back to those specs.
non_goals: Document every implementation detail before the project has real seams.
verification: Review starter specs for project-specific intent and ownership coverage.
Run the rebuild command after replacing placeholders.

delta.desired: Product intent and architecture boundaries are explicit and stable.
delta.current: Starter docs need project-specific content.
delta.closure: Replace placeholders with concrete intended behavior and ownership boundaries.

TASK-002.title: Map code ownership into spec hierarchy
TASK-002.summary: Refine the inferred boundary docs until wiki/system mirrors the repo's real ownership seams without creating doc sprawl.

goal.outcome: wiki/system reflects real stable ownership seams in the repo.
acceptance: Each meaningful code area maps to one canonical owning spec.
Unnecessary inferred boundaries are removed or collapsed.
non_goals: Create a spec for every folder regardless of architectural value.
verification: Review inferred boundary docs against actual repo structure.
Run rebuild and inspect mapping/drift output.

delta.desired: Each meaningful layer or component has one canonical owning spec.
delta.current: Setup can infer first-pass boundaries, but humans still need to confirm or reshape them.
delta.closure: Add, remove, or rewrite inferred spec folders until they match real stable ownership seams.

TASK-003.title: Keep roadmap as freshest delta log
TASK-003.summary: Move drift and plan tracking into structured roadmap tasks instead of separate prose buckets.

goal.outcome: Tracked delta lives in roadmap tasks instead of scattered prose.
acceptance: Active implementation gaps are represented by roadmap tasks.
Users can resume task work from Pi surfaces without editing roadmap JSON manually.
non_goals: Maintain separate plan and drift documents for the same live work.
verification: Inspect generated roadmap view after rebuild.
Confirm roadmap tasks cover current active delta.

delta.desired: Roadmap is single current queue for closing docs-to-code gaps.
delta.current: Teams often spread gaps across plans, drift notes, and chat.
delta.closure: Convert each active mismatch or sequence into a scoped roadmap task.

TASK-004.title: Expose CodeWiki capability manifest and think-code boundary
TASK-004.summary: Define the typed CodeWiki capabilities that Pi runtimes such as think-code can call without bypassing .codewiki semantics, including optional executor/fallback boundaries.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki exposes a clear capability/transaction surface for reads, task mutations, evidence appends, graph rebuilds, and validated wiki edits while composing with think-code as an optional bounded executor.
acceptance: Runtime policy spec names the capability classes CodeWiki owns.
Manifest shape includes capability name, args schema, result schema, read/write classification, and audit fields.
Raw writes to generated state remain out of scope for external runtimes.
Plan covers behavior when think-code is not installed.
Plan covers project .codewiki read access and write delegation rules.
non_goals: Implement a general sandbox.
Duplicate telemetry or eval extension behavior.
Make think-code a hard dependency before the integration contract is stable.
verification: Review against think-code capability boundary task
Run codewiki state refresh after spec changes
Compare intended behavior in Pi sessions with and without think-code installed

delta.desired: External runtimes call CodeWiki through explicit semantic capabilities and can optionally delegate bounded execution to think-code.
delta.current: CodeWiki has a gateway script and transaction schema, but no formal capability manifest or optional think-code integration boundary.
delta.closure: Specify the manifest, fallback behavior, and follow-up implementation tasks.

TASK-005.title: Split CodeWiki skills into focused outer and inner workflows
TASK-005.summary: Replace the single monolithic CodeWiki skill with a package-level router plus focused CodeWiki skills for planning, task execution, research, and verification.

goal.outcome: Agents load only the CodeWiki workflow instructions needed for the current job while preserving shared CodeWiki invariants.
acceptance: A small `codewiki` router skill states package invariants and when to load each focused skill.
Focused skills exist for `codewiki-documentation`, `codewiki-implementation`, `codewiki-research`, and `codewiki-verify`.
Skill descriptions are precise enough for Pi progressive disclosure and do not duplicate large workflow text.
All focused skills preserve `.codewiki/kb` as canonical intent and roadmap/evidence as machine-managed operational truth.
non_goals: Install unrelated global skills as part of this repo task.
Replace CodeWiki tools or roadmap schema.
verification: Run package typecheck/tests after skill/package changes.
Use Pi startup or package listing to confirm focused skills are discoverable.
Review each skill for overlap and conflicting instructions.

delta.desired: CodeWiki package exposes composable workflow skills optimized for planning, execution, research, and verification.
delta.current: One CodeWiki skill contains all guidance and is growing into a monolith.
delta.closure: Split skill files and keep a compact router/invariants skill.

TASK-006.title: Define CodeWiki outer planning loop
TASK-006.summary: Specify the automatic outer loop that turns user intent into wiki knowledge updates and roadmap tasks before task execution begins, with user approval only at decision checkpoints.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki has an automatic planning workflow for clarifying intent, gathering evidence when needed, updating knowledge, and creating roadmap tasks.
acceptance: Planning loop is documented as clarify/inspect/research-if-needed/update-knowledge/create-roadmap-tasks with automatic progression where safe.
Research is explicitly outside normal task execution and supports knowledge claims.
Roadmap tasks are created only after intended behavior and acceptance criteria are clear enough.
User-facing commands/prompts can start the outer loop, but the user does not manually trigger each stage.
The loop pauses for user input only when ambiguity, product choice, destructive change, or approval policy requires it.
non_goals: Implement every planned task.
Turn CodeWiki roadmap into an external issue tracker.
Remove human approval from material product or architecture decisions.
verification: Review generated roadmap tasks for spec links, outcomes, acceptance, non-goals, and verification.
Run codewiki state refresh and confirm roadmap summary remains healthy.
Dry-run a planning request and confirm stages advance without manual command choreography.

delta.desired: Planning automatically creates durable knowledge and an executable roadmap before implementation starts.
delta.current: Workflow guidance exists but outer-loop responsibilities are not isolated or automated.
delta.closure: Add planning-specific skill/spec guidance and ensure new tasks follow it with minimal user command choreography.

TASK-007.title: Define CodeWiki task execution inner loop
TASK-007.summary: Specify the automatic per-task loop that uses think-code context packets, implementation, local checks, fresh verification, and evidence before closure without requiring user-triggered verification commands.

goal.outcome: Each roadmap task has an automatic implement→verify loop grounded in task context, tests, and evidence.
acceptance: Task loop is documented as load task/context, create compact context with think-code when useful, implement, run local checks, automatically invoke fresh verification when ready, append evidence, then close/block/follow-up.
Local verification and fresh-context verification have distinct responsibilities.
Task evidence captures checks run, files touched, issues, and verifier verdict.
Closure criteria require acceptance criteria and verification evidence, not just model confidence.
User command/manual trigger is treated as override/debug, not normal flow.
non_goals: Force TDD for every trivial documentation-only task.
Make think-code a hard dependency when unavailable.
verification: Run a sample task through the documented automatic loop or dry-run the workflow with an existing task.
Confirm task history/evidence records contain enough data to resume or audit work.
Confirm task closure cannot skip required verifier stage unless policy explicitly allows it.

delta.desired: Task execution is automatic, evidence-backed, and resumable from implementation through verification.
delta.current: Implement→verify guidance exists but is not formalized as an automatic focused inner loop skill.
delta.closure: Add inner-loop skill/spec guidance and link it to CodeWiki task/session tools and automatic verifier stage.

TASK-008.title: Model research evidence as support for wiki knowledge
TASK-008.summary: Separate external/source research from task execution so research evidence supports `.codewiki/kb` claims rather than becoming part of every implementation loop.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki distinguishes research evidence for intended knowledge from execution evidence for task closure.
acceptance: Research workflow records cited findings as support for knowledge updates and design decisions.
Task execution loop treats research as optional only when uncertainty blocks implementation or claims need support.
Evidence schema or conventions distinguish source/research evidence from checks/execution evidence.
The research skill explains when to use web/code research versus local code inspection.
non_goals: Require web research for routine code changes.
Create a separate ADR system outside `.codewiki/kb`.
verification: Review evidence entries produced by sample research and sample task execution for clear classification.
Run codewiki state refresh and confirm no generated state is hand-edited.

delta.desired: Research strengthens canonical knowledge without bloating task execution.
delta.current: Research is described as optional evidence but not modeled as a distinct workflow.
delta.closure: Add research-specific workflow guidance and evidence classification.

TASK-009.title: Add automatic fresh-context verifier agent workflow
TASK-009.summary: Design and implement an automatic verifier stage that spawns a read-only agent when task execution reaches verification, while retaining a manual command only as an override/debug entrypoint.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki automatically verifies task completion through a separate read-only Pi agent as part of the inner loop before closure.
acceptance: Inner loop invokes verifier automatically when a task is ready for closure or explicit verification evidence is missing.
Verifier stage accepts a task id internally and builds a compact verification brief from task context, linked specs, diff/status, and acceptance criteria.
Verifier runs in a separate Pi process or SDK/RPC session with read-only tools plus allowed check commands.
Verifier returns deterministic JSON verdict with pass/fail/block, checks, issues, and rationale.
Parent CodeWiki flow appends verifier output as task evidence without automatically mutating code.
Auto-close is gated by verifier pass plus local checks plus no unresolved issues and user/project policy.
Manual `/wiki-verify` or equivalent, if present, is a debug/override entrypoint rather than the primary workflow.
non_goals: Give verifier write/edit/apply authority.
Replace human review for security-sensitive or product-sensitive changes.
Require verifier for every tiny docs typo when policy says skip.
verification: Create tests or smoke scripts for verifier trigger selection, brief generation, and verdict parsing.
Manual smoke: run automatic verifier stage against a known passing and known failing task fixture.
Confirm verifier cannot write files or mutate roadmap directly.

delta.desired: Task closure automatically uses independent fresh-context verification rather than relying on users to trigger a command.
delta.current: Skill guidance recommends fresh verification, but no automatic verifier stage exists.
delta.closure: Add automatic verifier stage, verifier prompt/protocol, evidence append path, and optional manual override.

TASK-010.title: Add CodeWiki architecture review workflow adapted from deep-module heuristics
TASK-010.summary: Adapt deep-module and shared-language architecture review ideas into a CodeWiki-aware workflow for finding seams, locality problems, and roadmap-worthy refactors.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can run architecture reviews that produce knowledge updates and roadmap tasks rather than ad hoc refactor suggestions.
acceptance: Workflow uses CodeWiki vocabulary and `.codewiki/kb` instead of `CONTEXT.md`/ADR assumptions.
Review rubric includes module/interface/implementation, seam, adapter, locality, leverage, and deletion test.
Findings become either knowledge clarifications, roadmap tasks, or explicitly deferred non-goals.
Architecture review does not propose interfaces before presenting friction candidates and asking user to pick.
non_goals: Create a separate ADR tree outside `.codewiki/kb`.
Automatically refactor architecture during review.
verification: Run the workflow on the CodeWiki repo and produce at least one candidate or documented no-op result.
Confirm any resulting tasks link to owning specs and acceptance criteria.

delta.desired: Architecture improvement becomes a first-class CodeWiki planning input.
delta.current: General architecture heuristics are useful but not adapted to CodeWiki's knowledge/roadmap model.
delta.closure: Add CodeWiki-specific architecture review skill or section and validate against the repo.

TASK-011.title: Record curated global agent skills decision
TASK-011.summary: Document which general engineering skills should be installed or adapted globally for Pi without conflicting with CodeWiki-specific workflows.

goal.outcome: There is a durable CodeWiki decision record for global Pi skills versus CodeWiki-specific skills.
acceptance: Global candidates are tdd, diagnose, grill-me, zoom-out, and write-a-skill.
Excluded or adapt-first skills are documented with reasons.
Notes include Pi package security and skill discovery behavior.
No global Pi settings are changed without explicit user approval.
non_goals: Install all external skills immediately.
Replace CodeWiki roadmap with external issue skills.
verification: Review decision text against Pi docs and existing global packages.
Confirm no repo-specific conventions are forced globally.

delta.desired: Global skills are curated with compatibility rationale.
delta.current: Decision exists only in chat.
delta.closure: Add durable decision guidance before installation or packaging.

TASK-012.title: Reduce canonical surface and projection write amplification
TASK-012.summary: Projection firewall first slice completed: canonical/generated contract documented and refresh=false paths now avoid projection locks/rebuilds where safe.

goal.outcome: Normal CodeWiki updates write only canonical truth while generated graph/status/lint/roadmap views are rebuilt as disposable projections.
acceptance: Canonical and generated file classes are documented in system knowledge.
Generated projection files are marked or described as tool-owned/read-only for agents.
At least one mutator path avoids unnecessary generated projection writes unless refresh is explicitly requested.
codewiki_state still returns current status after refresh.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: Remove all generated files from the repository.
Implement the architecture diagram projection.
Rewrite the whole roadmap storage model.

delta.desired: Agents see a small canonical write surface and projection files become generated, tool-owned caches.
delta.current: Task/session workflows can rewrite multiple roadmap/state/graph/lint/status projection files, increasing token cost and drift surface.
delta.closure: Projection firewall documented and first write-amplification reduction implemented.

TASK-013.title: Decouple closed-task retention from projection rebuild
TASK-013.summary: Closed-task retention no longer depends on generated projection rebuilds; refresh=false close path preserves archive retention.

goal.outcome: Closing tasks can enforce roadmap archive retention through the canonical task mutation path without depending on rebuild side effects.
acceptance: Task close with closed_task_limit=0 archives the closed task even when projection refresh is false.
Generated projection rebuild remains available and does not become the only retention mechanism.
Smoke coverage verifies archive retention for refresh=false or equivalent minimal-write close path.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: Remove legacy rebuild retention immediately.
Change archive file format.
Change default retention policy.

delta.desired: Retention belongs to canonical roadmap mutation; generated projection rebuilds are no longer required for correct archive preservation.
delta.current: Roadmap archive retention is enforced by rebuild, so codewiki_task keeps refresh=true by default for compatibility.
delta.closure: Task close retention is implemented in extension code and covered by smoke tests.

TASK-014.title: Capture CodeWiki v2 operating model and roadmap
TASK-014.summary: CodeWiki v2 decisions are now durable wiki knowledge and mapped to executable roadmap tasks.

goal.outcome: The wiki records the v2 canonical/view model, product split, memory policy, subagent/agency/think-code roles, and sanitation/versioning policy as roadmap-backed intent.
acceptance: V2 decisions are captured in canonical knowledge docs, not only chat history.
Product knowledge is split or prepared for users, stories, and surfaces.
The system knowledge names the canonical/view boundary and view consumption model.
Roadmap tasks cover v2 migration, views, architecture graph, subagents, agency, think-code, and sanitation/release policy.
codewiki_state reports the new roadmap without drift.
non_goals: Implement the full v2 storage migration.
Render architecture diagrams in the status panel.
Rename all code-level projection terminology immediately.

delta.desired: V2 direction is captured as wiki truth and mapped to concrete roadmap tasks.
delta.current: V2 direction exists in chat but not yet as durable wiki knowledge or executable roadmap tasks.
delta.closure: Knowledge docs and roadmap tasks reflect the agreed v2 operating model.

TASK-015.title: Implement v2 wiki layout and migration
TASK-015.summary: V2 layout compatibility is implemented: `.codewiki/**` is generated alongside legacy read models and bootstrap/rebuild understand the v2 views root.

goal.outcome: New and migrated repos use canonical knowledge/tasks/evidence plus generated views under the v2 structure without breaking legacy paths.
acceptance: V2 target paths are represented in config/templates.
Legacy `.codewiki/roadmap/queue.json` and current generated files remain readable during migration.
Migration or bootstrap creates the v2 directory shape safely.
Rebuild can populate v2 views while preserving current tool behavior.
non_goals: Remove all legacy paths in one release.
Rewrite every tool call to the final v2 paths immediately.

delta.desired: V2 layout makes canonical truth and `.codewiki/**` read models explicit.
delta.current: Current layout mixes canonical files and generated read models under several top-level `.codewiki/` paths.
delta.closure: Bootstrap/migration and rebuild understand v2 paths with legacy compatibility.

TASK-016.title: Bake product split and lexicon into templates
TASK-016.summary: Starter templates now include the v2 product split and shared lexicon, with smoke coverage for the new files.

goal.outcome: New wikis start with focused product knowledge and a shared lexicon rather than one large product overview.
acceptance: Bootstrap templates include lexicon, users, stories, and surfaces docs.
Product overview is short navigation plus boundaries.
Smoke tests cover expected starter files.
Existing clients docs link cleanly to the product split.
non_goals: Delete all client surface docs.
Implement system architecture graph generation.

delta.desired: Every new CodeWiki repo gets the v2 product/lexicon structure.
delta.current: This repo has the new product split, but package templates still need to adopt it.
delta.closure: Templates and smoke tests reflect product split and lexicon.

TASK-017.title: Render architecture graph in status panel
TASK-017.summary: Update the CodeWiki status panel to render the generated architecture view and show component or flow details when selected.

goal.outcome: Humans and agents can inspect system architecture visually through the status panel and zoom into component or flow detail cards.
acceptance: Status panel renders the generated architecture graph or a fallback graph view.
Selecting a component opens linked component markdown/details.
Selecting a flow opens linked flow markdown/details.
Health colors use strict legend semantics from roadmap/drift/view state.
Panel degrades gracefully when architecture views are stale or absent.
non_goals: Make the panel a full diagram editor.
Hand-edit Mermaid in the UI.

delta.desired: Architecture graph becomes an interactive navigation surface.
delta.current: Status panel focuses on status and roadmap, not architecture graph navigation.
delta.closure: Panel can render graph and detail cards from generated views.

TASK-018.title: Implement context memory policy in skills and loops
TASK-018.summary: Teach agents when to use session RAM, views, canonical docs, subagents, and ThinkCode so default workflows minimize repeated token load.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki skills and loops default to tiny views and targeted expansion instead of reloading raw wiki state into the parent session.
acceptance: Planning, task, verification, research, and architecture skills describe RAM vs persistent memory rules.
Default flow starts from status view and recommended next reads.
Agents track loaded view revisions and avoid rereading unchanged views when possible.
Task and outer-loop instructions explain when to spawn subagents or ThinkCode.
non_goals: Implement full agency scheduler.
Build new view schemas from scratch if TASK-018 is not done.

delta.desired: Agent workflow policy explicitly optimizes context-window usage.
delta.current: Skills mention compact state but do not fully model session RAM, views, subagents, and ThinkCode together.
delta.closure: Skills and loop prompts encode the memory policy.

TASK-019.title: View schema generator for status queue drift and briefs
TASK-019.summary: Define and generate the small, revisioned read models agents and UI consume for status, roadmap queue, task context, drift, product brief, system architecture, and recent evidence.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki exposes compact generated views that route agents to the right next read without loading raw wiki state.
acceptance: Views include revision metadata, stale flags where applicable, size budgets, and recommended next reads.
Status, roadmap queue, task context, drift, product brief, system architecture, and recent evidence views are defined.
`codewiki_state` can expose or consume the views without increasing default token load.
Views remain rebuild-owned and read-only to agents.
non_goals: Build status panel graph interaction.
Implement agency autonomy.

delta.desired: Views become the default optimized read models for inner and outer loops.
delta.current: Generated state exists but is not organized as purpose-specific v2 views with routing contracts.
delta.closure: View schemas and generation are implemented and verified.

TASK-020.title: Architecture manifest and Mermaid view generator
TASK-020.summary: Add canonical system architecture graph support with component and flow docs, then generate agent-readable and Mermaid architecture views from it.

goal.outcome: System architecture is represented as a structured canonical graph with component and flow detail docs and generated visual/read views.
acceptance: `system/architecture.json` owns component nodes and information-flow edges.
`system/components/*.md` detail component cards.
`system/flows/*.md` detail important cross-component paths.
Rebuild generates a Mermaid architecture view and agent-readable system architecture view.
Graph validation detects missing component/flow docs or orphan docs.
non_goals: Implement status panel click interactions in this task.
Make Mermaid the source of truth.

delta.desired: Structured graph truth plus generated architecture views.
delta.current: System architecture is mostly prose; generated graph state is not a first-class architecture source.
delta.closure: Architecture graph and Mermaid generation work with validation.

TASK-021.title: Subagent JSON worker contracts
TASK-021.summary: Define and implement compact JSON contracts for verifier, researcher, planner, architecture reviewer, and view auditor subagents.

goal.outcome: Context-heavy or bias-sensitive CodeWiki work can run in fresh subagent sessions and return bounded results to the parent agent.
acceptance: Verifier, researcher, planner, architecture reviewer, and view auditor roles have documented input/output contracts.
Parent agent remains responsible for canonical writes.
Subagent outputs are compact JSON with pass/fail/block or proposal semantics as appropriate.
Existing fresh verifier behavior aligns with the shared subagent model.
non_goals: Let subagents freely mutate `.codewiki/` canonical truth.
Replace all parent-agent work with subagents.

delta.desired: Subagents become reusable context-management workers.
delta.current: Fresh verifier exists, but broader subagent roles and contracts are underexplored.
delta.closure: Contracts and at least one additional subagent workflow are implemented or stubbed with tests.

TASK-022.title: Agency modes budgets and stop conditions
TASK-022.summary: Add bounded observe, maintain, and work agency modes that use views, subagents, and canonical writes safely.

goal.outcome: Users can run CodeWiki autonomously within explicit budgets and modes without losing alignment with wiki truth or project safety.
acceptance: Agency supports observe, maintain, and work modes.
Each cycle reads status, handles stale views, selects inner or outer loop action, records evidence, and stops on budget/risk/ambiguity.
Budgets include max cycles, wall time, writes, subagents, and risk level.
Push/version/archive behavior is gated by policy and checks.
non_goals: Run unbounded autonomous coding.
Bypass user approval for risky or destructive work.

delta.desired: Agency is a bounded CodeWiki loop with safe modes and stop conditions.
delta.current: Agency is an idea but not a fully defined or implemented autonomous workflow.
delta.closure: Agency command/tool contract and initial implementation are covered by tests.

TASK-023.title: ThinkCode query executor integration
TASK-023.summary: Use ThinkCode for ad hoc programmatic context creation, view validation, graph checks, and staged edits without making it CodeWiki's memory layer.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can delegate bounded query/ETL and validation tasks to ThinkCode while preserving CodeWiki semantic write boundaries.
acceptance: ThinkCode role is documented in skills and runtime guidance.
At least one workflow uses ThinkCode for compact context creation or graph/view validation when available.
Fallback path works when ThinkCode is unavailable.
ThinkCode writes remain staged or flow through CodeWiki capabilities/transactions.
non_goals: Make ThinkCode required for CodeWiki.
Let ThinkCode edit generated views directly.

delta.desired: ThinkCode acts as a policy-bound programmatic context and validation executor.
delta.current: ThinkCode is documented as future runtime support but not integrated into view/context workflows.
delta.closure: Integration seam and first use case are implemented with fallback.

TASK-024.title: Sanitation checkpoints and git history policy
TASK-024.summary: Keep hot wiki state small by relying on git for full history and storing only compact semantic archives and release checkpoints.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki distinguishes hot current truth, generated views, compact semantic history, and full git recovery.
acceptance: Closed task archive stores compact semantic summaries with commit/release links rather than full raw context by default.
Release checkpoint schema records version, git sha, canonical digest, view schema version, and closed task summaries.
Docs and skills describe commit/push/version boundaries.
Views exclude cold history unless explicitly requested.
non_goals: Duplicate full git history inside `.codewiki/`.
Automatically push or version without policy gates.

delta.desired: Sanitation keeps agent hot context small while preserving semantic memory and git recovery.
delta.current: Retention exists but archives and release/version boundaries are not fully aligned with git as full history.
delta.closure: Archive/checkpoint policy is implemented and documented.

TASK-025.title: Audit read model wording in README skills and tools
TASK-025.summary: Align public package language so generated read models are called views and source-of-truth files are called canonical truth.

goal.outcome: User-facing and agent-facing text use the same v2 vocabulary for views and canonical truth.
acceptance: README describes views and the canonical/view boundary.
Skills tell agents to consume views and avoid editing views.
Tool descriptions mention rebuilding views where relevant.
non_goals: Change storage paths.
Implement new view schemas.

delta.desired: Public and agent instructions use view vocabulary consistently.
delta.current: Some package text still says projection, derived state, or generated state in places where v2 wants view terminology.
delta.closure: README, skills, and tool descriptions match the lexicon.

TASK-026.title: Decouple ThinkCode from CodeWiki memory policy
TASK-026.summary: Remove wording that frames ThinkCode as an enforced CodeWiki runtime and describe it only as optional compatible tooling.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki operates independently as a Pi package while allowing ThinkCode or other tools to integrate optionally.
acceptance: Skills do not require ThinkCode for CodeWiki workflows.
Code/task/verifier prompts frame programmatic context executors as optional/replaceable.
Knowledge and lexicon describe ThinkCode as compatible external tooling, not CodeWiki runtime.
Checks pass.
non_goals: Change ThinkCode package behavior.
Remove all mentions of ThinkCode where optional integration is useful.

delta.desired: CodeWiki is standalone and stack-agnostic; ThinkCode is optional external tooling enforced only by its own skill.
delta.current: Recent memory-policy wording overstates ThinkCode as CodeWiki context/query executor.
delta.closure: Wording changed to optional/compatible tool language.

TASK-027.title: Simplify status panel tabs and header
TASK-027.summary: Update CodeWiki status panel UI to show repo-only header, Home/Product/System/Board tabs, deprecate Agents/Channels tabs, and render system Mermaid architecture with selectable components.

goal.outcome: Status panel presents user-focused Home, Product, System, and Board tabs while backend roadmap terminology remains unchanged.
acceptance: Header shows only the repo name.
Home shows overall traffic-light project status and meaningful factors influencing it.
Product shows users, stories, and surfaces entries only.
System renders the architecture Mermaid diagram and supports selectable components whose details open from component markdown.
Roadmap tab is renamed to Board in UI only; backend roadmap naming remains unchanged.
Agents and Channels tabs are removed or deprecated from the status panel UI.
non_goals: Rename backend roadmap/task concepts.
Implement external agent/channel systems.
verification: manual/status panel smoke or existing rendering smoke coverage where available

TASK-028.title: Stabilize CodeWiki refactor after partial DDD migration
TASK-028.summary: TASK-028 close: TASK-033 gate recorded fresh pass evidence; stabilizing refactor is complete.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki has a green baseline before further architecture migration proceeds.
acceptance: `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` pass.
`codewiki_state` works from the Pi tool path or the failure is captured with a blocking root cause.
Current duplicate module sets are inventoried with a keep/delete/move decision.
No broad folder moves happen before the baseline is verified.
non_goals: Do not implement MCP, Codex, or Claude adapters.
Do not complete the full DDD migration in this task.
Do not delete compatibility shims without proving all imports moved.
verification: Run a CodeWiki state read or targeted equivalent for this repo.
Inspect git diff for unrelated rewrites.

delta.desired: Verified stable baseline with known drift and a safe migration path.
delta.current: Baseline is green: typecheck, tests, architecture check, pack dry-run, and live codewiki_state refresh pass. Pi surface is adapter-isolated, duplicate engine path is removed, and current seams are documented.
delta.closure: Baseline checks pass; current migration drift is documented; remaining future work lives in TASK-30/TASK-032.

TASK-029.title: Add architecture boundary checks and characterization coverage
TASK-029.summary: TASK-029 verified and closed during TASK-033 baseline gate.

goal.outcome: Refactor work is protected by automated import-boundary checks and behavior characterization tests.
acceptance: A script checks that domain does not import Pi, Node I/O, application, infrastructure, adapters, or legacy core.
Application and infrastructure boundary rules are encoded with transitional exceptions where needed.
Smoke/characterization coverage exercises bootstrap, state read, task mutation, session mutation, and rebuild behavior at the current public surface.
Package scripts expose the checks so future agents can run one command before and after migration slices.
non_goals: Do not enforce final no-legacy-import rules until migration tasks remove shims.
Do not rewrite production architecture as part of adding checks.
verification: Run the new architecture check script.
Verify the check fails on an intentional forbidden import in local reasoning or unit fixture if practical.

delta.desired: Boundary violations become visible immediately and behavior changes are caught before broad moves.
delta.current: Architecture check is implemented and wired into npm test. Smoke coverage exercises package load, bootstrap, state/task/session behavior, rebuild, status UI expectations, and pack contents.
delta.closure: Guardrail scripts and tests pass and are documented in package scripts.

TASK-031.title: Move Pi surface into adapters and retire legacy core structure
TASK-031.summary: TASK-031 verified and closed during TASK-033 baseline gate.

goal.outcome: Repository layout matches the target ports/adapters architecture without duplicate engine/core surfaces.
acceptance: `src/index.ts` is a thin Pi extension entrypoint that delegates registration to `src/adapters/pi`.
Pi tools, commands, and TUI code live under `src/adapters/pi`.
There is one engine/rebuild implementation path, with old duplicate `src/engine` removed or converted to a temporary shim before deletion.
`contracts.ts` exports stable public contracts from canonical locations, not legacy `core` internals.
Legacy `core`, `tools`, `commands`, `ui`, and duplicate `engine` shims are deleted only after import-boundary checks deny their use.
non_goals: Do not change public command/tool names.
Do not add non-Pi adapters beyond a tiny proof adapter if separately tasked.
verification: Run architecture boundary check with final no-legacy rules enabled.
Inspect package `files` and Pi extension discovery still include correct resources.

delta.desired: Clear folder ownership with Pi-specific code isolated and legacy structure removed.
delta.current: Pi tools, commands, and UI live under src/adapters/pi; index.ts is thin; duplicate infrastructure/engine path and legacy src/tools/src/commands/src/ui folders are gone; transitional core shims remain intentionally for follow-up application extraction.
delta.closure: No duplicate migration artifacts remain and all public Pi behavior still passes tests.

TASK-032.title: Prove the adapter seam with a minimal CLI surface
TASK-032.summary: Cancelled in roadmap transition after KB boundary refactor: minimal CLI proof is a future adapter option, not active work until the API/application seam is stable and there is a concrete access-surface need.

goal.outcome: A second simple adapter validates that CodeWiki workflows are no longer Pi-bound.
acceptance: CLI exposes a minimal state read command and one task operation using application use cases.
CLI code does not duplicate domain/task/session logic from Pi tools.
Tests prove Pi and CLI paths share application behavior.
Findings from the CLI proof update future MCP/Codex/Claude adapter tasks if needed.
non_goals: Do not build a full CLI product.
Do not implement MCP server, Codex extension, or Claude Code integration in this task.
verification: Run minimal CLI command against a temp fixture if packaged.

delta.desired: A tiny second adapter proves the ports/application seam with minimal complexity.
delta.current: Only Pi is a real adapter. Application/core seams are improved, but no minimal CLI adapter exists and no CLI fixture test proves shared application behavior.
delta.closure: Unblock by either implementing the minimal CLI proof or replacing it with a more relevant adapter proof task after application use-case extraction completes.

TASK-033.title: Checkpoint stable refactor baseline before next roadmap
TASK-033.summary: TASK-033 baseline gate closed: stable dogfood checkpoint recorded with honest pass/block outcomes.

goal.outcome: After TASK-040 hardens the verifier, current TASK-028 through TASK-032 are verified, closed, and recorded as a stable dogfooding baseline.
acceptance: TASK-040 is done and `task-close` verification gateway is active.
TASK-028 through TASK-032 have fresh verification evidence from the hardened verifier and are either closed or explicitly blocked.
`npm test` passes on the stable baseline.
A release checkpoint records git SHA, canonical digest, view schema version, and closed-task summaries.
non_goals: Do not implement new sprint, skill, UI, agency, or runtime features in this gate.
Do not rely on the previous weak verifier path for baseline closure.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck` if available/required for the touched code.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm green health.

TASK-034.title: Align CodeWiki skill package with compiler and API contracts
TASK-034.summary: Update packaged CodeWiki skills and workflow assets so they route work through feedback, documentation, implementation, validation, graph, roadmap, build, and agency contracts without stale roadmap-work-item or nested-KB assumptions.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki packaged skills reflect the flat KB, builds-as-briefs model, gated agency, validation gateway, and active-roadmap truth boundaries.
acceptance: Skills route ambiguous intent to feedback and accepted changes to documentation builds before implementation work.
Planning/task skills use documentation_build plus roadmap work item language instead of legacy requirements-packet briefs.
Verify/view-audit skills align with validation gateway and graph state-machine contracts.
Skills avoid stale references to deleted nested system docs and legacy operating-model paths.
Workflow guidance stays progressive-disclosure and does not bypass API/roadmap/build/validation semantics.
non_goals: Do not copy external skill conventions that duplicate CodeWiki knowledge, roadmap, build, validation, or graph truth.
Do not make skills hand-edit generated graph or roadmap shards.
Do not implement runtime code refactors beyond skill/docs alignment.
verification: grep skills for stale nested KB paths and roadmap-work-item terminology
Review skill router against system/api.md, compilers.md, validation-gateway.md, agency.md
Run existing smoke/tests if skill package loading is affected.

delta.desired: Skills are aligned with the new CodeWiki compiler/API/graph/agency model.
delta.current: Skills still contain some old requirements-packet and legacy operating-model language.
delta.closure: Skill docs aligned with current KB contracts; implementation build 2026-05-10-task-034-skill-alignment.json validates closure.

TASK-036.title: Introduce sprint planning model
TASK-036.summary: Cancelled in roadmap transition after KB boundary refactor: a sprint layer would overcomplicate the current model. Roadmap should stay active work truth, with builds as briefs and graph/gated agency handling routing. Revisit only if active-roadmap + graph workflow proves insufficient.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki supports `roadmap -> sprints -> tasks` while keeping task execution atomic and verifiable.
acceptance: Sprint semantics are documented: future sprints contain outcomes/spec links/risks, next sprint contains tasks.
Roadmap schema and generated views can represent sprint summaries and next-sprint tasks.
Planning workflow creates tasks only for the next sprint.
Sprint closure requires integration verification and drift review.
non_goals: Do not build a full project-management suite.
Do not force existing flat roadmap users through a breaking migration without compatibility.
verification: Verify old flat roadmap fixtures remain readable or have a safe migration path.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and inspect queue/status views.

TASK-037.title: Build standalone read-only CodeWiki UI
TASK-037.summary: Cancelled in roadmap transition after KB boundary refactor: standalone read-only UI is deferred until graph/API/active-roadmap semantics are stable and a concrete visual UI need is accepted. Product visual UI docs remain as expectations, not immediate implementation work.

goal.outcome: Humans can inspect CodeWiki status, roadmap, product/system views, and Mermaid architecture without running Pi.
acceptance: A standalone read-only UI surface is designed or implemented without importing Pi SDK/TUI code.
UI consumes generated views as its only data source for initial slice.
Mermaid architecture renders in an environment suited to diagrams.
Pi status panel remains compatible or delegates shared formatting where useful.
non_goals: Do not add canonical writes in the first external UI slice.
Do not make UI the access layer for agents; agents should use views, CLI, MCP, or native adapters.
verification: Manually open/read fixture views in the standalone UI or snapshot-render output.
Confirm no Pi imports cross into standalone UI modules.

TASK-038.title: Implement trigger-based agency controller
TASK-038.summary: Replace the current agency planning stub with a bounded trigger-based controller whose names match user-facing moments such as task end, sprint end, roadmap end, and budget end.

goal.outcome: Users can choose understandable agency triggers and safe autonomy budgets instead of opaque autonomy levels or OpenClaw-derived agency-loop terminology.
acceptance: Agency trigger names align with user-facing events: task-end, sprint-end, roadmap-end, and budget-end.
Each trigger maps to bounded observe/maintain/work behavior with explicit budgets and stop conditions.
Cron-like operation is limited to read-only or low-risk drift/audit maintenance unless explicit policy permits more.
Agency can route to feedback/documentation/implementation/validation only when trigger, budget, and risk policy allow it.
Old agency-loop terminology is removed from live KB, source, skills, README, and smoke tests.
non_goals: Do not implement unbounded autonomous coding.
Do not push, release, archive, or mutate high-risk state without explicit approval and green checks.
Do not keep queue.json as a generated queue lens; graph owns derived queue order.
verification: Add or update smoke coverage for agency tool registration and trigger-to-action planning.
Verify live KB/source/docs contain no old agency-loop terminology and queue.json is not regenerated.

delta.desired: Agency planning is trigger-aware, graph-driven, and free of OpenClaw-derived agency-loop terminology. Graph owns derived queue order; roadmap owns work truth.
delta.current: planAgency accepts trigger parameter; codewiki_agency is the Pi-facing tool; state builders expose agency lanes; queue.json generation removed; KB clarifies agency and graph/roadmap boundaries.
delta.closure: Agency terminology and trigger-based planning implemented; queue.json removed; implementation build 2026-05-10-task-038-agency-controller.json validates closure.

TASK-039.title: Provide optional ThinkCode runtime adapter
TASK-039.summary: Cancelled in roadmap transition after KB boundary refactor: ThinkCode remains an optional agent helper, not core CodeWiki roadmap work. Recreate only if a concrete adapter/runtime need appears after API, graph, and agency semantics stabilize.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can prefer ThinkCode when available but remains usable from any agent runtime through views, CLI/gateway, MCP/future adapters, or native tools.
acceptance: Runtime docs describe ThinkCode as optional, not required memory or execution substrate.
Adapter contract identifies where `think-code.policy.json` lives and how staged writes are validated.
Fallback paths are documented for native CodeWiki gateway/views when ThinkCode is unavailable.
The design supports future Codex and Claude Code adapters without Pi-specific assumptions.
non_goals: Do not make ThinkCode a hard dependency.
Do not let ThinkCode bypass CodeWiki canonical write rules or mutate generated views directly.
verification: Validate gateway manifest still advertises CodeWiki capabilities independent of ThinkCode.
Check skills mention optional fallback behavior clearly.

TASK-040.title: Harden verification gateway before dogfood checkpoint
TASK-040.summary: Implement a first-class CodeWiki verification gateway/router before relying on existing verifier evidence to close the stable refactor baseline and start dogfooding.

goal.outcome: Task closure and future sprint/release/drift checks use a robust verification gateway instead of ad hoc verifier prompts or parent-session confidence, using hardened context packs from the view/context-compiler work where needed.
acceptance: Define a `codewiki.verify`-style internal gateway/router contract with profiles, briefs, policies, and verdict schema.
Implement the `task-close` profile first and route `codewiki_task action="close"` through it.
Run deterministic preflight before semantic verification, including task goal fields, linked path existence, evidence/check presence, and view freshness where applicable.
Consume or define a compact verifier context pack instead of relying on ad hoc task prompt assembly.
Invoke semantic verification in a separate fresh read-only process/session/subagent with a complete CodeWiki verifier rubric and compact task brief.
Parse and validate verifier JSON strictly; malformed output returns `block` with evidence.
Add golden or smoke coverage for `pass`, `fail`, `block`, and malformed-output cases.
Ensure verifier cannot mutate canonical truth or generated views; parent remains sole writer for evidence and lifecycle changes.
non_goals: Do not implement all profiles in the first slice beyond contract stubs or documented routing.
Do not make verification depend on ThinkCode or any single runtime.
Do not close baseline refactor tasks using the old weak verifier path.
Do not solve the full sprint/roadmap context compiler in this task.
verification: Run `npm run typecheck` if required by touched TypeScript.
Manually exercise or unit-test `task-close` verifier outcomes for pass/fail/block.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm green health.

TASK-041.title: Review and improve generated views into a token-efficient context compiler
TASK-041.summary: Audit and redesign generated `.codewiki/**` as lightweight routing and observability read models, then benchmark prebuilt packs versus bounded programmatic exploration to choose the most token-efficient context strategy for task/sprint/roadmap work.

goal.outcome: Generated views become a first-class context routing layer that supports compact role-specific packs, graph slices, and optional bounded programmatic exploration without confusing observability with verification gates.
acceptance: Document the context-access model: views provide map/cached routes, bounded sandbox/programmatic tools provide microscope-style exploration, and verifier gateways remain the only quality gate.
Benchmark or prototype four context strategies for representative task work: prebuilt pack only, progressive disclosure only, sandbox/programmatic exploration only, and hybrid pack plus sandbox.
Define role-specific seed/context pack contracts for at least `grill`, `plan`, `build`, and `verify`, emphasizing minimal routing metadata instead of large precomputed context dumps.
Define typed graph-slice views for task/sprint/roadmap scopes with `core`, `supporting`, `watch`, and `excluded` tiers plus edge reasons and confidence (`explicit` vs `inferred`).

Define horizontal and vertical observability signals only: knowledge/task/evidence/view alignment and intent → knowledge → roadmap → code → checks/evidence alignment; do not make observability a pass/fail gate.
Define an acceptance/evidence matrix that maps criteria to spec paths, code paths, checks, and evidence for verifier consumption.
Represent token budget as generator/test metadata (`estimated_tokens`, `max_tokens`, `over_budget`) rather than instructions the agent must maintain manually.
Implement or prototype the smallest useful `task.build`, `task.verify`, and `task.graph` views needed by the hardened verification gateway, without blocking future sprint/roadmap expansion.
Add tests or smoke checks proving generated context views stay deterministic, stale-aware, and within size guarantees.
non_goals: Do not introduce embeddings, vector search, or external search infrastructure in the first slice.
Do not build the standalone UI; only shape views so UI and agents can consume them later.
Do not replace canonical knowledge, roadmap, evidence, or verifier verdicts with generated views.
Do not make observability itself a closure gate; verification consumes observability but decides separately.
Do not hand-edit generated view files.
verification: Run view rebuild and confirm `.codewiki/status.json` or task contexts point to the new context entrypoints where appropriate.
Run `npm run typecheck` if TypeScript files change.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm health stays green.
Manually inspect one generated task seed/graph pack for token efficiency, recommended next reads, and acceptance/evidence mapping.

TASK-042.title: Make wiki-resume honor persisted task focus across sessions
TASK-042.summary: Fix /wiki-resume without an explicit task so fresh Pi sessions use persisted CodeWiki focus/resume state instead of falling back to the first active roadmap task.

goal.outcome: A new Pi session can run /wiki-resume and pick the intended persisted focus task, matching codewiki_state guidance.
acceptance: /wiki-resume with no explicit task prefers a valid persisted focus/resume task when present.
Generated status/resume views agree with roadmap queue and persisted focus instead of reporting a different next task.
Smoke coverage proves a later persisted focus on TASK-033 wins over earlier active TASK-029 ordering.
Explicit /wiki-resume TASK-### continues to override persisted focus.
non_goals: Do not redesign full session compaction or multi-agent scheduling.
Do not close unrelated verification tasks.
verification: Run codewiki_state refresh=true and confirm green health.

delta.desired: Fresh sessions resume the same focused roadmap task from persisted CodeWiki state.
delta.current: New sessions lose Pi branch-local focus, so /wiki-resume can choose the first active roadmap task such as TASK-029 while codewiki_state suggests TASK-033.
delta.closure: Resume selection and generated views are aligned, covered, and verified.

TASK-044.title: Generate graph-first CodeWiki index
TASK-044.summary: TASK-044: Graph-first index with build/test/validation nodes, verifier removed, close flow simplified.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki rebuild produces `.codewiki/index_graph.json` as the primary generated graph and treats any remaining status/queue outputs as cached graph lenses.
acceptance: `.codewiki/index_graph.json` is generated by rebuild rather than manually copied.
Graph contains nodes/edges for knowledge docs, roadmap tasks, builds, validation reports, and code/test manifests where available.
Status/queue surfaces either read from the graph or are explicitly documented cached graph queries.
Legacy task-context view generation is removed or isolated behind a compatibility path.
non_goals: Remove all compatibility files in the same task.
Redesign the status panel UI.
verification: npm test or targeted smoke coverage for rebuild/state

TASK-045.title: Add compiler build and validation report schemas
TASK-045.summary: TASK-045: compiler build and validation report schemas complete.

goal.outcome: Compiler builds and validation reports have explicit schemas, write paths, and validation rules.
acceptance: Schemas exist for feedback_build, documentation_build, implementation_build, and validation_report.
Writers or transaction paths can create build artifacts without hand-editing JSON.
Passing validation can be transient while fail/block/policy-required reports persist under `.codewiki/validation/**`.
Generated graph indexes build and validation artifacts.
non_goals: Implement full multi-agent orchestration.
Change task close policy beyond schema integration.
verification: schema/unit tests for accepted and rejected artifacts

TASK-047.title: Stabilize CodeWiki self-hosting baseline
TASK-047.summary: Make CodeWiki stable enough to dogfood by aligning canonical rebuild, event/state sources, tests, and validation before continuing graph/compiler/role refactors.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki has one trusted self-hosting baseline: TypeScript rebuild is canonical, event/state sources agree, tests pass, and verifier closure can be trusted again.
acceptance: Configured rebuild path uses the TypeScript rebuild engine, not src/engine/rebuild.ts or external command aliases.
Legacy legacy rebuild script is removed, archived outside runtime/package scope, or clearly non-runtime with no config/test/docs dependency.
Canonical event/state source for resume/session focus is documented and used consistently by rebuild, codewiki_state, /wiki-resume, and tests.
TASK-042 acceptance passes under the same canonical rebuild path used by codewiki_state and task close verifier.
Smoke tests cover fresh-session persisted focus, generated status/queue agreement, and absence of legacy rebuild dependency.
README, templates, KB, roadmap refs, and gateway scripts no longer advertise legacy external as the starter/runtime rebuild path.
codewiki_state refresh=true returns green and fresh verifier agrees with npm run typecheck + npm test.
non_goals: Do not implement full TASK-044 graph-first index beyond what is needed for a stable baseline.
Do not implement TASK-045 build schemas or TASK-046 tester/builder roles in this stabilization task.
Do not redesign product/system KB layout.
Do not broaden scope to unrelated UI polish.
verification: grep -R for legacy external rebuild/runtime stale refs in README, templates, KB, source, scripts, roadmap
Attempt to close TASK-042 after stabilization and confirm verifier passes.

delta.desired: One TypeScript canonical CodeWiki runtime supports reliable dogfooding and future refactors.
delta.current: Repo truth is split: docs and decisions say TypeScript/graph/compiler model, but config, scripts, tests, generated state, and verifier still see legacy external/events legacy paths.
delta.closure: TASK-042 and future CodeWiki tasks can be validated against the same rebuilt state that tests and agents use.

TASK-048.title: Create per-compiler KB files and feedback compiler skill
TASK-048.summary: TASK-048: Per-compiler KB files and feedback compiler skill created.

goal.outcome: Each compiler has its own KB spec file under .codewiki/kb/system/compilers/ and the feedback compiler has a dedicated codewiki-feedback SKILL.md file.
acceptance: feedback.md KB file exists describing feedback compiler responsibilities
documentation.md KB file exists describing documentation compiler responsibilities
implementation.md KB file exists describing implementation compiler responsibilities
validation-gateway.md KB file exists describing validation gateway responsibilities
codewiki-feedback/SKILL.md exists as dedicated feedback compiler skill
Router skill references feedback compiler
Lint warning for missing skills/codewiki-feedback is resolved
non_goals: Change compiler logic or tool implementations
Refactor existing skill files

TASK-049.title: Implement feedback build workflow and writer
TASK-049.summary: TASK-049: Feedback build writer implemented with lifecycle metadata and smoke coverage.

goal.outcome: Agents can run the feedback loop and produce a durable but transient accepted feedback_build through CodeWiki tooling or documented command flow.
acceptance: A feedback build schema or typed contract exists
Feedback_build artifacts include lifecycle state and TTL/cleanup metadata
A writer path/tool/command creates feedback_build artifacts under .codewiki/builds/feedback/
The codewiki skill explains the feedback loop execution path without manual JSON hand-editing
Smoke coverage proves feedback_build creation works in a temp repo
Feedback loop failure mode from this session is covered by a regression or documented guard
non_goals: Implement full documentation compiler automation
Implement the full reconciliation state machine
Require SQLite or external storage
verification: Manual run or smoke fixture creates a feedback_build artifact with lifecycle metadata

delta.desired: Feedback loop is executable and durable enough for handoff, but builds remain transient lifecycle-managed artifacts.
delta.current: Feedback compiler is documented, but no first-class writer path exists; assistant attempted to emit a tool call into chat and no artifact was created until manual correction.
delta.closure: Add schema/writer/workflow docs/tests so feedback_build creation is reliable and lifecycle-aware.

TASK-050.title: Implement graph-backed state machine and reconciliation gateway
TASK-050.summary: TASK-050: graph-backed reconciliation state machine and gateway routing implemented.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can derive alignment state from the graph and recommend the correct loop entry point: feedback, documentation, or implementation.
acceptance: Graph schema includes alignment/state fields for layer nodes and edges
State surface reports reconciliation next action from graph state
Drift classification routes to feedback when intent is missing, documentation when truth must update, implementation when code/tests must change
Reconciliation gateway is documented as controller/gateway, not a fourth compiler
Tests cover at least one downward flow and one upward code-to-doc reconciliation scenario
non_goals: Make SQLite the primary store
Replace the three compilers
Store raw event history
verification: codewiki_state refresh=true shows healthy graph state

delta.desired: Graph acts as rebuildable state tracker over canonical layers and gateways route into the right compiler loop.
delta.current: Graph indexes nodes/edges but does not yet represent lifecycle/alignment state or reconciliation routing.
delta.closure: Add graph/state fields, routing logic, docs, and tests for state-machine reconciliation.

TASK-076.title: Restructure project filesystem and dogfood boundary
TASK-076.summary: Tidy CodeWiki house by moving package code to root src, consolidating public skills, clarifying that .codewiki is dogfood state rather than package source, and removing the confusing top-level infrastructure layer.

goal.outcome: Project file structure matches the agreed root-src, single-skill, dogfood-state boundary without adding new behavior.
acceptance: Pi extension entry resolves from src/index.ts and package manifest/files no longer require extensions/codewiki.
Only skills/codewiki/SKILL.md is a public CodeWiki skill; loop/playbook instructions live under that skill as supporting markdown.
Project Pi system prompt appends a clear .codewiki dogfood-state-not-source-code boundary.
Tests and docs reference moved source paths consistently.

Top-level src/infrastructure does not exist; gateway policy, graph rebuild orchestration, knowledge parsing, and local runtime helpers live under src/application/** while harness/web translation stays under src/adapters/**.
scripts/ contains only active dev/CLI scripts; package smoke lives under tests/smoke and stale transaction duplication is removed.
Architecture/type checks pass or blockers are documented.
Task-specific acceptance coverage lives under tests/tasks/TASK-076/ with trace metadata.
non_goals: Add new CodeWiki product behavior.
Change compiler semantics beyond file/storage path naming required by the restructure.
Publish, push, or release.

delta.desired: Runtime code lives under root src, one public CodeWiki skill owns loop/playbook docs, agents see .codewiki as dogfood state only, and package source uses domain/application/adapters without a top-level infrastructure layer.
delta.current: Runtime code has been moved to root src, public CodeWiki skill surface has been consolidated, docs/prompts now identify .codewiki as dogfood state rather than package source, former infrastructure files have moved under application/gateway, application/graph, application/knowledge, and application/local, and scripts/ now contains only active dev/CLI wrappers.
delta.closure: File moves, imports, package metadata, tests, docs, prompt boundary, domain/application/adapters layer split, and script/test placement are aligned; final review/closure can proceed after independent validation.

TASK-077.title: Prevent cross-layer alignment drift
TASK-077.summary: Superseded by SPRINT-003: TASK-077 was a container/umbrella task, not self-contained executable work.

goal.outcome: Coordinate the alignment-hardening sprint and close only after child tasks implement executable prevention for vertical drift, horizontal drift, dogfood/source confusion, audit evidence, gateway policy, and publication proof.
acceptance: TASK-078 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-079 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-080 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-081 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-082 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-083 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-084 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
TASK-085 is closed with passing checks and evidence.
CodeWiki state is green with no unresolved reconciliation drift after the sprint.
Build, roadmap/task, schema, state, and graph APIs write change_type as canonical metadata.
Readers preserve backwards compatibility for existing change_class builds/tasks where practical.
Canonical change_type values are product, system, task, and code; security/audit/publication/maintenance are not primary type values.
Generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes remain exempt from accepted-build requirements through traceability exemption/non-semantic mode metadata.
Docs, skill guidance, and tests use change_type terminology consistently.
non_goals: Do not treat this umbrella task as implementation evidence for child tasks.
Do not close until child tasks complete or are explicitly deferred with accepted feedback.
Do not reopen TASK-082.
Do not force new accepted builds for generated/runtime/mechanical-only graph refreshes.
Do not remove compatibility for existing TASK-082 artifacts if a low-cost alias is possible.
verification: codewiki_state reports green health and no reconciliation drift
All child task verification checks pass

delta.desired:
CodeWiki has executable alignment contracts and sprint tasks that prevent the same drift classes before close, validation, or publication.
Refinement: Additional outcome: CodeWiki uses change_type as the canonical semantic traceability field with a compact product/system/task/code taxonomy and a separate generated/runtime/mechanical exemption mechanism.
Refinement: Semantic traceability uses clear change_type terminology and four target buckets, while exemption metadata separately controls build requirements.
delta.current:
Manual audit found drift after docs, roadmap, generated views, package source, validation reports, and scripts had already diverged.
Refinement: TASK-082 introduced change_class with mixed taxonomy values that conflate target domain, workflow/risk labels, and build-traceability exemptions.
delta.closure:
All sprint child tasks are closed or explicitly deferred with accepted feedback and graph/gateway/audit checks pass.
Refinement: Source, docs, tests, builds, and validation accept change_type canonical metadata with change_class compatibility and no build-per-file tax.

TASK-084.title: Model publication and content-proof alignment
TASK-084.summary: Represent commits, tree SHAs, package digests, archive ledgers, PR/release text, and safe-to-push evidence as publication/content-proof layer refs in builds, graph, and gateway policy.

goal.outcome: Publication gates and graph reconciliation distinguish validation attestations from immutable content proof and block publication when proof or claim alignment is missing.
acceptance: Builds and validation reports can record working-tree digest, tree SHA, commit SHA, package digest, archive ledger, remote ref, and publication claim refs.
Graph includes publication/content-proof nodes or compact refs and flags missing proof for publication claims.
Publication validation requires immutable content proof and aligned commit/package/PR/release text according to policy.
Tests cover pre-commit validation vs publication proof distinction.
non_goals: Do not implement automatic remote push/release unless already supported by policy.

delta.desired: Publication/content-proof task closes only after fresh validation confirms immutable publication proof behavior.
delta.current: Publication proof policy is implemented, but fresh validation and any remaining publication alignment scope are pending; status reopened from auto-done evidence transition.
delta.closure: Close after validation and final review of TASK-084 acceptance criteria.

TASK-078.title: Define executable alignment graph contract
TASK-078.summary: Implement graph reconciliation rules for vertical alignment, graph/gateway precedence, validation attestations, and content-proof nodes from the accepted alignment model.

goal.outcome: Graph reconciliation v2 represents required layer edges, validation attestations, audit evidence, and content proof without treating graph state as canonical truth.
acceptance: Graph output distinguishes canonical source refs, validation attestations, audit evidence, and content proof refs.
Reconciliation items cover missing feedback->docs->planning->roadmap->tests/code->validation->publication edges for active work.
Closed-task historical fail/block validations do not route current drift unless policy keeps them hot.
Tests cover graph green-vs-drift cases and stale validation/content-proof precedence.
non_goals: Do not make graph canonical truth.
Do not implement the full audit engine in this task.
verification: codewiki_state shows expected reconciliation output for test fixture

delta.desired: Graph is a deterministic vertical alignment state machine usable as required gateway context.
delta.current: Graph contract implementation and fresh validation are complete.
delta.closure: Closed after implementation build and fresh validation pass with working_tree_digest proof.

TASK-087.title: Migrate source to domain-led CodeWiki layers
TASK-087.summary: Restructure CodeWiki source so domain concepts power DDD and application code contains compilers, gateways, state engine, and agent-facing tools over those domain concepts.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki source follows the documented dependency direction: adapters/UI/skills call application tools, application compilers/gateways/state-engine use domain concepts, and domain owns task/roadmap/session/build/validation/state semantics.
acceptance: Domain modules exist for task, roadmap, session queue, build, validation, and state concepts, with product semantics moved out of broad application modules where practical.
Application exposes agent-callable tool APIs that Pi tools, future adapters, skill helpers, and optional CLIs can call without owning semantics.
Application compiler modules exist for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation flows, even if migration starts with wrappers around existing implementations.
Application gateway modules are separated by validation profile and do not collapse into compiler build production.
State-engine naming owns generated state/graph rebuild/query behavior while graph remains the generated representation.
Dependency checks or tests prevent domain importing application/adapters/UI/skills/scripts and prevent application importing adapters/UI/skills/scripts.
non_goals: Do not change user-facing behavior except import/path compatibility required by the refactor.
Do not remove legacy compatibility exports until downstream adapters/tests are migrated.
verification: source-owned architecture/audit check covers dependency direction

delta.desired: Domain concepts lead the architecture, application orchestrates compiler/gateway/state/tool workflows, and adapters/UI/skills are thin surfaces.
delta.current: Many domain concepts live in broad src/application modules and generated graph/state naming is mixed across layers.
delta.closure: Layer folders and imports match documented DDD target with tests/guards and compatibility wrappers where needed.

TASK-088.title: Move web UI out of adapter layer
TASK-088.summary: Move browser UI source from adapter ownership to src/ui/web while keeping adapters focused on harness/protocol launch and translation.

goal.outcome: Browser UI code lives under src/ui/web and delegates semantics to application tools, while adapters only launch/connect or translate harness/protocol concerns.
acceptance: src/ui/web owns browser UI rendering/server assets currently under src/adapters/web.
Pi adapter launch commands delegate to UI/application APIs without importing UI semantics as adapter business logic.
Web UI does not import Pi SDK/TUI packages, hosted CDNs, or adapter-only APIs.
Docs/frontmatter code_paths are updated from current src/adapters/web to src/ui/web only after the path exists.
Package exports, smoke tests, and pack dry-run include the moved UI assets correctly.
non_goals: Do not redesign the UI feature set during the move.
Do not make web UI a hosted service or agent harness adapter.
verification: manual /wiki-ui or equivalent smoke if available

delta.desired: Web is treated as product UI under src/ui/web, not an agent harness adapter.
delta.current: Browser UI source currently lives under src/adapters/web for historical reasons.
delta.closure: UI files move to src/ui/web, launch adapters still work, docs/frontmatter update, and smoke/package checks pass.

TASK-090.title: Codify alignment drift fixture matrix
TASK-090.summary: Add deterministic fixture cases for alignment drift detection in fixture-only test assets.

goal.outcome: Known alignment drift classes have deterministic fixture coverage independent of task-boundary enforcement.
acceptance: Fixture cases cover vertical drift, horizontal drift, dogfood/source confusion, stale generated output, missing accepted build, missing audit evidence, and invalid publication proof.
Each failing fixture asserts a specific issue kind or reconciliation item.
Clean fixture passes the same audit/graph check path.
Fixture runner is included in smoke or feature tests.
non_goals: Do not change task-boundary enforcement.
Do not depend on network or remote Git state.

delta.desired: Fixture matrix covers known alignment drift classes.
delta.current: Task-boundary enforcement slice is complete; fixture matrix remains separate testing work.

SPRINT-001.title: Efficiency foundation
SPRINT-001.outcome: CodeWiki hot state becomes scoped, cheap, and parallel-safe.

SPRINT-002.title: Status, Graph, and Diff Table UI
SPRINT-002.outcome: Users can inspect health, scope, graph relationships, feedback diff rows, and closure briefs without reading raw JSON.

SPRINT-003.title: Alignment hardening
SPRINT-003.outcome: CodeWiki prevents vertical drift, horizontal drift, dogfood/source confusion, missing audit evidence, and publication/content-proof drift before close, validation, or publication.
